channel.title: CyberNetSec - Policy and Compliance
channel.description: Latest policy and compliance threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: UK Fines Capita £14M for "Preventable" 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
The UK's Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) has levied a £14 million fine against outsourcing giant Capita for significant data protection failures related to a March 2023 data breach that impacted 6.6 million people. The ICO's investigation concluded the breach was 'preventable' and heavily cr...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: California Enacts Stricter Data Breach Law with 30-Day Notification Deadline
item.description:
California has enacted Senate Bill 446, a new law that significantly shortens the data breach notification timeline for businesses. Organizations must now inform affected California residents of a data breach involving unencrypted personal information within 30 calendar days of its discovery. This a...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Regulatory, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: New York Inflation Refund Program Exploited in Phishing Scams
item.description:
The New York State Department of Taxation and Finance is warning residents about phishing and smishing (SMS phishing) campaigns that are exploiting a legitimate state inflation relief program. Scammers are sending fraudulent messages claiming that recipients must submit personal and payment informat...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: North Korean IT Worker Fraud Scheme Expands, Targeting 5,000 Companies
item.description:
A sophisticated North Korean scheme using fraudulent IT worker personas to infiltrate companies has expanded into a massive global operation. According to a report from October 11, 2025, researchers have identified over 130 fake identities used in more than 6,500 job interviews with approximately 5,...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Incident Response, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: US Cyber Threat Sharing Law 'CISA 2015' Expires, Creating Potential Intelligence Gap
item.description:
The Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015 (CISA 2015), a foundational U.S. law that provided liability protections to encourage private companies to share cyber threat data with the government, expired on October 1, 2025. Amidst a government shutdown and a block by Senator Rand Paul, lawmake...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Financial Firms Tie CEO Pay to Cyber Performance Amid Budget Hikes, Moody's Finds
item.description:
A new report from Moody's indicates a significant shift in how financial and insurance firms are managing cyber risk. Companies are increasing cybersecurity spending, with nearly half dedicating 8% or more of their IT budget to cyber. Governance is also strengthening, as 40% of firms now link CEO co...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Expert Advice on Securing Critical Infrastructure with Limited Budgets
item.description:
In a recent podcast, cybersecurity expert Chetrice Romero from Ice Miller provided guidance for leaders responsible for protecting critical infrastructure, particularly those facing limited budgets. The discussion covered common cyber and physical threats to utilities, the need for scalable and resi...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Industrial Control Systems, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK's NCSC Warns of 'Alarming' Rise in Cyberattacks, Doubling in Past Year
item.description:
The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) revealed in its 2025 annual review that it managed 204 "nationally significant" cyberattacks over the past year, more than double the 89 incidents from the previous year. The agency attributed the alarming surge to increasing threats from state-sponsore...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Atos Partners with Qevlar AI to Deploy "Virtual SOC Analyst"
item.description:
On October 7, 2025, the global digital transformation and cybersecurity firm Atos announced a strategic partnership with Qevlar AI. The collaboration will integrate Qevlar's 'Virtual SOC Analyst,' an agentic AI technology, into Atos's global network of 17 Security Operations Centers (SOCs). The goal...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: Signal Threatens to Exit EU Market if "Chat Control" Mass Surveillance Bill Passes
item.description:
Meredith Whittaker, the president of the Signal Foundation, has declared that the encrypted messaging service will withdraw from the European Union if the controversial 'Chat Control' legislation is enacted. The proposed law, which faces a critical vote on October 14, would mandate that communicatio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →
